title: Lecture
description: A single lecture or course entry in the awesome-lectures collection.

binaryRating.description: Bayesian like-count rating (Algorithm A). rating = Bayesian mean likes/day (float ≥ 0), weight = cumulative days active (int).

scaleRating.description: Bayesian 0–10 scale rating (Algorithm B). rating = Bayesian mean (0–10), spread = Bayesian std deviation, weight = cumulative vote count (int).

title.description: Title of the lecture or course.

urls.description: One or more URLs pointing to the lecture video or playlist.

speakers.description: Names of the speaker(s) or presenter(s).

embed.description: Whether the video can be embedded (e.g. YouTube embed allowed).

extra.description: Free-text description, abstract, or additional context.

year.description: Year the lecture was recorded or published.

startsAt.description: Offset in seconds at which the relevant content starts.

learnings.description: What a viewer can learn from this lecture. Each element is an object whose keys describe a learning outcome and whose values are scale ratings.

liked.description: Aggregate viewer like signal (binary — no rating scale, just positive signals).

beginnerExpertSpectrum.description: Where on the beginner–expert spectrum the content sits (0–10 scale). Low = very beginner-friendly, high = expert-level.

worthWatchingWithoutVideo.description: How worthwhile the lecture is as audio-only (0–10 scale).
